_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning how the language we use both expresses and creates our personal and social identities.

_source.w: We are learning how the language we use both expresses and creates our personal and social identities.
y: Language is not just a tool for communication — it actively shapes who we are and how others see us.
Understanding this connection helps us make more intentional choices about how we present ourselves.
This awareness helps us recognise how identity is constructed in texts and in everyday interactions.
eg: We analyse how slang, dialect and code-switching express belonging to particular social groups.
We discuss how our language choices change depending on who we are with and what identity we want to project.
We examine how characters in texts use language to establish their identity — accent, vocabulary, register.
We explore how language can reinforce or challenge stereotypes about different groups.
We reflect on our own language use and what it says about our identities.
We discuss how digital communication — emojis, abbreviations, memes — creates new forms of identity expression.
